steps.title: Identify the subject and the grammar rule
steps.content: The subject of the blank clause is “the word ‘lasso’” — singular. The parenthetical “(a rope)” is just a clarifying aside and has no effect on the subject’s number. The sentence states a permanent etymological fact (not a past event or ongoing action), so simple present tense is appropriate. A singular subject in simple present requires a verb ending in -s.

steps.title: Apply subject-verb agreement to each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — were deriving: Past progressive, plural — wrong in both number (plural) and tense/aspect (etymology is a standing fact, not a past ongoing action). ✗
  • Choice B — have derived: Present perfect, plural — wrong in number and the perfect aspect implies a recently completed change rather than a permanent origin. ✗
  • Choice C — derive: Simple present base form — correct tense and aspect, but plural; does not agree with the singular subject “the word ‘lasso’.” ✗
  • Choice D — derives: Singular simple present — agrees with the singular subject and correctly expresses a permanent etymological fact. ✓
D is correct.
